# Incursa.Quic

> AI-readable bootstrap for the `Incursa.Quic` repository. This file is non-authoritative. The prep docs and repo-local instructions should be treated as guidance, not as the source of truth.

`Incursa.Quic` is the scaffold repository for a managed QUIC library. Future RFC-derived requirement work should stay explicit, traceable, benchmarked, and test-heavy.

When working in this repository:

- Follow this authority order: `specs/` landing pages and gap tracker, `docs/requirements-workflow.md`, root docs, then `AGENTS.md`, then this file.
- Canonical SpecTrace artifacts are authored in sibling `.json` files, and the repository does not keep canonical sibling `.md` companions in the normal workflow.
- Record unresolved RFC questions in `specs/requirements/quic/REQUIREMENT-GAPS.md` before implementation.
- Keep requirements, architecture, work items, verification, tests, and benchmarks separate.
- For parsing, serialization, encoding, decoding, or packet-processing code, expect positive tests, negative tests, fuzz/property coverage, and benchmarks.
- Use stable file paths and relative links for repo-local Markdown.
- Do not infer missing protocol rules into code until the requirement slice exists.
- Before finishing a trace-surface change, run `pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 -Profiles core` and `dotnet tool run workbench -- --format json validate --profile core`.
- `Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1` downloads the upstream SpecTrace model schema from [incursa/spec-trace](https://github.com/incursa/spec-trace/raw/refs/heads/main/model/model.schema.json) unless you override `-SchemaUri`.

## Useful Starting Points

- [`README.md`](./README.md)
- [`docs/requirements-workflow.md`](./docs/requirements-workflow.md)
- [`docs/README.md`](./docs/README.md)
- [`docs/testing/README.md`](./docs/testing/README.md)
- [`specs/README.md`](./specs/README.md)
- [`specs/requirements/quic/README.md`](./specs/requirements/quic/README.md)

## Current State

- The library project is still empty.
- Build, packaging, and test scaffolding are already in place.
